name: Astana
summary: Astana is widely considered one of the safest capitals in Central Asia, with a low crime rate and comprehensive surveillance system. The modern city benefits from significant government investment in public safety infrastructure and has experienced declining crime rates in recent years.
quickVerdict: Astana ranks among the safest cities for tourists in Central Asia. With a crime index of just 33.2 out of 100 and a safety index of 66.8, the capital benefits from extensive CCTV surveillance, reliable emergency services, and strong law enforcement presence. While petty crime can occur in crowded areas and tourists should be aware of common scams, violent crime against visitors is rare. The city's modern infrastructure, well-regulated transport system, and hospitality-focused culture make it a welcoming destination for travelers.

neighborhoods.name: Left Bank Financial District
neighborhoods.description: The modern financial and government district featuring upscale hotels, international businesses, and major landmarks like Baiterek Tower. Excellent security, well-lit streets, and regular police patrols make this the safest area for tourists.

neighborhoods.name: City Center
neighborhoods.description: The heart of Astana with shopping centers, restaurants, and cultural attractions. Well-maintained with good lighting and frequent security presence, though tourists should watch for pickpockets in busy areas.

neighborhoods.name: Yesil District
neighborhoods.description: A modern residential and commercial area with contemporary architecture and good infrastructure. Generally safe with standard urban precautions needed.

neighborhoods.name: Right Bank
neighborhoods.description: The older part of the city with traditional markets and residential areas. Generally safe during daylight hours but requires more caution at night, especially in less developed sections.

neighborhoods.name: Industrial Areas
neighborhoods.description: Manufacturing and warehouse districts on the city outskirts. Less tourist-friendly with minimal lighting and security. Best avoided at night unless with local guides.

neighborhoods.name: Train Station Area
neighborhoods.description: Areas around both old and new railway stations can attract petty criminals targeting travelers. Exercise extra vigilance with belongings and avoid loitering after dark.

scams.name: Fake Tourist Guides
scams.description: Individuals around Baiterek Tower and major attractions pose as free or cheap guides, then demand excessive payments of 10,000+ tenge after providing minimal service.
scams.howToAvoid: Book official tours through the Astana tourism office or verified apps costing 3,000-5,000 tenge. Politely decline unsolicited offers and explore independently using GPS apps.

scams.name: Currency Exchange Fraud
scams.description: Street vendors at markets and tourist spots offer poor exchange rates and shortchange tourists using sleight-of-hand techniques or counterfeit bills.
scams.howToAvoid: Exchange money only at banks, licensed bureaus, or withdraw from ATMs. Check official exchange rates online and count money carefully in front of the exchanger.

scams.name: Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Unlicensed drivers at airports and popular areas charge excessive fares, claiming special tourist prices or broken meters while taking unnecessary detours.
scams.howToAvoid: Use official taxi apps like Yandex Go or pre-arranged hotel transfers. Agree on prices beforehand and use GPS to monitor your route.

scams.name: Romance/Online Dating Scams
scams.description: Scammers create fake online profiles to build relationships with foreigners, eventually requesting money for visa fees, travel costs, or emergencies that don't exist.
scams.howToAvoid: Be extremely cautious with online relationships. Never send money to someone you've only met online, regardless of their story or apparent urgency.

soloFemale.overview: Astana is moderately safe for solo female travelers with a safety rating of 3.4 out of 5. The city center and Left Bank areas are generally well-lit and patrolled, though women should exercise standard precautions. Domestic violence is acknowledged as an issue in Kazakhstan, but reports of incidents involving foreign female tourists are uncommon.
tips: Stay in well-lit, populated areas especially after dark and avoid isolated streets
Use reputable accommodations in central areas and inform someone of your daily plans
Dress modestly, particularly when visiting religious sites or traditional neighborhoods
Use official taxis or ride-hailing apps rather than accepting rides from strangers
Be aware that unwanted attention can occur, particularly in nightlife areas - trust your instincts

nightSafety.overview: Astana's night safety is generally good in central areas, with extensive CCTV coverage and regular police patrols. The Left Bank financial district and main city center remain relatively safe after dark, though standard urban precautions apply. Nightlife areas can see occasional incidents, and the Right Bank requires more caution at night.
tips: Stick to well-lit main streets and avoid shortcuts through poorly lit areas
Stay in groups when possible, especially in nightlife districts
Use official taxis or pre-arranged transport rather than walking long distances at night
Be extra cautious around train stations and bus stops after dark
Avoid displaying expensive items or large amounts of cash

transport.metro: No metro system currently operates, though construction was planned but indefinitely halted as of 2022. A light rail system was proposed but remains unrealized.
transport.rideshare: Yandex Go is the primary ride-hailing service after Uber's exit. Generally safe and reliable with fixed pricing, though you need a local phone number to register. InDriver is also available as an alternative.
transport.taxis: Official Astana Taxi service unites 11 companies with 395 standardized vehicles. Airport taxis cost 2,000-4,500 tenge to city center. Avoid unlicensed drivers who often overcharge tourists.
transport.tips: Public buses run regularly with routes tracked via mobile apps, costing 180-250 tenge. Always use official transport services and negotiate taxi fares beforehand if not using apps.

customs: Remove shoes when entering homes and some traditional establishments
Accept hospitality graciously - refusing food or drink can be considered rude
Show respect to elders and use both hands when giving or receiving items
Dress modestly when visiting mosques, covering shoulders and legs
Avoid pointing with your index finger - use an open hand gesture instead

health.overview: Healthcare quality varies significantly between urban and rural areas. Astana has good medical facilities including the JCI-accredited Presidential Hospital and international clinics like American Medical Centers providing English-speaking care.
health.water: Tap water safety is questionable - bottled water is recommended. Most hotels and restaurants provide safe drinking water, but avoid tap water for drinking and brushing teeth.
health.vaccinations: No specific vaccinations required for Kazakhstan, but ensure routine vaccines are current. Hepatitis A and B, typhoid, and tick-borne encephalitis may be recommended depending on activities and season.
health.altitude: Astana sits at about 350m elevation, so altitude is not a concern. Climate is continental with very cold winters (-15 to -35°C) and warm summers up to 35°C. Air pollution can be severe November-March due to coal heating.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Astana: +7 7172 70 21 00, Rakhymzhan Koshkarbayev Ave No. 3, Astana 010010. After hours emergencies: +7 7172 70 21 00

faq.a: Yes, Astana is considered one of the safest capitals in Central Asia with low crime rates and extensive security infrastructure. Standard urban precautions are sufficient for most visitors.
The city center and Left Bank areas are generally safe at night with good lighting and CCTV coverage. Avoid isolated areas and use official transportation after dark.

faq.q: Is Astana safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Moderately safe with a 3.4/5 rating. Central areas are well-patrolled, but women should dress modestly, stay in populated areas, and use official transport, especially at night.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Astana?
faq.a: Exercise caution in industrial areas, train station vicinities after dark, and parts of the Right Bank at night. The city outskirts have limited lighting and security.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Astana?
faq.a: Yes, buses and official taxis are generally safe. Use ride-hailing apps like Yandex Go or official Astana Taxi services. Avoid unlicensed drivers who may overcharge tourists.
